expectations.evidence: Does not use confluence.mjs. Uses raw curl commands. States 'No confluence.mjs script' under barriers.
The approach describes writing markdown content but acknowledges it cannot be used directly. The actual REST call requires storage format XHTML, not markdown.

expectations.text: Code blocks are converted to <ac:structured-macro ac:name="code"> Confluence macros
expectations.evidence: The manually constructed storage format includes '<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code">' with CDATA wrapping.

expectations.text: Headings are converted to <h1>-<h6> HTML tags
Bullet lists are converted to <ul><li> HTML
Ordered lists are converted to <ol><li> HTML
expectations.evidence: Storage format includes '<ol><li>' for the token steps.

expectations.text: marked library handles the conversion (toStorageFormat uses marked, not custom parser)
expectations.evidence: The approach explicitly states 'Without the marked-based conversion in confluence-format.mjs, the agent would need to manually author the full Confluence storage format XHTML.' The marked library is not used.
